development.title: Development
development.description: Want to contribute to Board Games Tracker? Here's how to set up a local development environment.
development.step1Description: Clone the repository and install the dependencies:
development.step2Description: Set up the database and run the application:
development.guidelinesDescription: Please follow the existing code style and conventions. Make sure all tests pass before submitting a pull request.

translations.title: Translations
translations.description: Board Games Tracker supports multiple languages. You can help translate the application into your language.
translations.howToDescription: Translation files are located in the boardgametracker.client/public/locales folder. Each language has its own folder with JSON translation files.
stepsItems: Copy the en-US folder and rename it to your language code
Translate all values in the JSON files

bugsFeatures.title: Bugs / Features
bugsFeatures.description: Found a bug or have a feature request? We'd love to hear from you!
bugsFeatures.bugsDescription: Please open an issue on GitHub with a clear description of the bug, steps to reproduce, and any relevant screenshots.
bugsFeatures.featuresDescription: Have an idea for a new feature? Open a GitHub issue with the feature request label and describe what you'd like to see.

logging.title: Logging
logging.description: Board Games Tracker uses structured logging to help you debug issues.
logging.configurationDescription: Set the LOGLEVEL environment variable to control the logging level. Available levels are debug, info, warn, and error.
logging.viewingDescription: You can view the application logs using Docker:
